evidence_text: The reported system size was 108,000 lines of C# code.
source_excerpt: The abstract states the infrastructure was developed “during construction of a 108,000-line C# distributed system.”
note: The paper reports this as project scale context, not as a comparative benchmark.
caveat: The paper reports this as project scale context, not as a comparative benchmark.

evidence_text: The proposed infrastructure has three components: a hot-memory constitution, 19 specialized agents, and a cold-memory knowledge base of 34 documents.
source_excerpt: The abstract describes “a three-component codified context infrastructure” consisting of “(1) a hot-memory constitution,” “(2) 19 specialized domain-expert agents,” and “(3) a cold-memory knowledge base of 34 on-demand specification documents.”
note: This is the paper’s main architectural decomposition.
caveat: This is the paper’s main architectural decomposition.

evidence_text: The architecture assigns different loading strategies to each tier: constitution always loaded, specialist agents invoked per task, and knowledge-base documents retrieved on demand.
source_excerpt: Section 3 says the architecture has “three tiers, each with a distinct loading strategy and update frequency: hot memory (the constitution, always loaded), domain specialists (agents, invoked per task), and cold memory (the knowledge base, retrieved on demand).”
note: This is a direct statement of the system’s operating mechanism.
caveat: This is a direct statement of the system’s operating mechanism.

evidence_text: The constitution uses trigger tables to route tasks to specialized agents based mainly on which files are being modified.
source_excerpt: Section 3.1.1 states: “The constitution embeds trigger tables that route tasks to the appropriate specialized agent (Tier 2) based on observable signals—primarily which files are being modified.”
note: This describes a concrete routing mechanism inside Tier 1.
caveat: This describes a concrete routing mechanism inside Tier 1.

evidence_text: The paper states that over half of each project agent specification was project-domain knowledge rather than behavioral instructions.
source_excerpt: In Section 3.2, “over half of each specification’s content is project-domain knowledge (codebase facts, formulas, code patterns, known failure modes) rather than behavioral instructions.”
note: The source presents this as an intentional design choice in agent construction.
caveat: The source presents this as an intentional design choice in agent construction.

evidence_text: The knowledge base was served through an MCP server that exposed five search tools, including find_relevant_context(task) and suggest_agent(task).
source_excerpt: Section 3.3.1 says the knowledge base is served through an MCP server that “provides five search tools” and lists “find_relevant_context(task)” and “suggest_agent(task)” among them.
note: This is a concrete implementation detail for retrieval and routing support.
caveat: This is a concrete implementation detail for retrieval and routing support.

evidence_text: The current retrieval implementation used keyword substring matching rather than semantic retrieval.
source_excerpt: Section 3.3.1 states: “The current implementation uses keyword substring matching; semantic retrieval is discussed in Section 5.3.”
note: This bounds the retrieval mechanism and signals an explicit limitation relative to stronger retrieval approaches.
caveat: This bounds the retrieval mechanism and signals an explicit limitation relative to stronger retrieval approaches.

evidence_text: The project was developed across 283 sessions over 70 days of part-time work and 148 commits.
source_excerpt: Table 3 reports “Development period 70 days (part-time),” “Total commits 148,” and “Development sessions 283.”
note: These are project activity metrics, not controlled-performance measures.
caveat: These are project activity metrics, not controlled-performance measures.

evidence_text: The final context infrastructure totaled about 54 files and 26,200 lines, equal to 24.2% of code size.
source_excerpt: Table 3 lists “Total context 54 / ~26,200 / 24.2%” and breaks this into the constitution, specialized agents, and knowledge-base documents.
note: The paper later states this ratio is descriptive for this project, not a target.
caveat: The paper later states this ratio is descriptive for this project, not a target.

evidence_text: The author states that the 24.2% knowledge-to-code ratio should not be treated as a target or general finding.
source_excerpt: Section 4.2 says: “The knowledge-to-code ratio of 24.2% reflects this project’s complexity and domain, not a target or finding.”
note: This is an explicit caution against overgeneralizing one project’s ratio.
caveat: This is an explicit caution against overgeneralizing one project’s ratio.

evidence_text: The interaction dataset included 2,801 human prompts, 1,197 agent invocations, and 16,522 autonomous agent turns.
source_excerpt: Section 4.3 states: “The interaction dataset comprises 2,801 human prompts, 1,197 agent invocations, and 16,522 autonomous agent turns across these sessions.”
note: The source also reports 19,323 total interactions and about 9.9 human prompts per session.
caveat: The source also reports 19,323 total interactions and about 9.9 human prompts per session.

evidence_text: About 13% of sessions used a structured plan-execute-review workflow, while about 87% were ad hoc.
source_excerpt: Section 4.3 says: “The orchestration protocols supported two modes: structured sessions (~13%) following a plan-execute-review workflow ... and ad-hoc sessions (~87%) involving direct implementation or debugging.”
note: This is a session-pattern distribution, not an effectiveness comparison.
caveat: This is a session-pattern distribution, not an effectiveness comparison.

evidence_text: In the save-system case study, the specification was referenced in 74 sessions and 12 agent conversations, and the paper reports no save-related bugs across five later persistence features.
source_excerpt: Section 4.4.1 states that save-system.md was “the most-referenced specification in the project, appearing in 74 sessions and 12 agent conversations,” and that five subsequent persistence-related features “were implemented by agents with access to this specification,” after which “Coordination across 74 independent sessions over four weeks produced no save-related bugs.”
note: This is a case-study observation, not a controlled causal test.
caveat: This is a case-study observation, not a controlled causal test.

evidence_text: In the deterministic RNG debugging case, replacing a time-bucket hash input with a synchronized shot counter resolved the desynchronization bug.
source_excerpt: Section 4.4.4 says the agent concluded that “using time as an input to the combat RNG hash was fundamentally counterproductive” and that “Replacing the time-bucket parameter with a synchronized shot counter resolved the bug.”
note: The source presents this as an observed debugging outcome in one case study.
caveat: The source presents this as an observed debugging outcome in one case study.

evidence_text: Specification staleness was reported as the primary failure mode, and the evaluation was limited to a single developer on a single project without controlled experiments.
source_excerpt: The abstract states the infrastructure was developed “during construction of a 108,000-line C# distributed system.”
note: The paper reports this as project scale context, not as a comparative benchmark.
caveat: The paper reports this as project scale context, not as a comparative benchmark.
